headline: Temenggor Lake Poaching Ring Seized 312 Protected Animals
context: PERHILITAN's Operation Khazanah in November 2025 dismantled a poaching ring at Temenggor Lake, Perak, seizing 312 protected animals including pangolins, sun bears, and hornbills.

cards.big: 312 protected animals were seized in a single raid at Temenggor Lake.
cards.sub: The poaching ring had operated for an estimated 3 years across 4 base camps in the forest reserve.

cards.big: 8 suspects were arrested. All were released on RM2,000 bail.
cards.sub: Maximum sentence under the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 is RM100,000 or 3 years. Actual sentences average RM8,000.

cards.big: Pangolin trafficking is worth an estimated RM180M annually in Malaysia.
cards.sub: PERHILITAN's entire wildlife enforcement budget is RM24M — 13% of the trade's annual value.

cards.big: Temenggor is the second-largest contiguous forest in Peninsular Malaysia.
cards.sub: It has 3 permanent ranger stations for 148,000 hectares — one ranger per 49,000 hectares.

cards.big: RM2,000 bail for trafficking protected species signals that wildlife crime is a misdemeanour, not a serious offence.
Wildlife crime must be treated as organised crime — with proportionate sentences, asset seizure, and intelligence-led enforcement. RM8,000 fines do not deter a RM180M trade.
